home.heading: Welcome
content: I'm a front-end engineer with over 20 years of experience building web applications for companies like Vanguard, Bank of America, and Best Egg. I currently lead a team at Best Egg working on customer-facing applications.

home.heading: About me
content: I've been working in web development for most of my adult life. It started as a hobby in the 1990s and became a career after I graduated from the University of Delaware with a degree in Business Administration (Finance/Accounting). I've held various roles and worked on many different types of projects, and I still enjoy the constantly-evolving nature of web development.

Currently, I work with Angular and TypeScript at Best Egg, a fintech company in the personal loan space. My past work has included building design systems at Vanguard, creating email campaigns for Bank of America, developing SharePoint sites for DuPont and Chemours, and building web-based interfaces for scientific instruments at Agilent.

When I'm not coding, you'll probably find me playing drums or bass. I'm the father of two great kids, an avid cyclist and runner, and a craft beer enthusiast. I often trade sleep for late-night TV, listening to vinyl, or tinkering with side projects.

home.heading: A brief detour
content: From 2005 to 2008, I took a break from full-time tech work to pursue music. My band, The Crash Motive, signed with Wind-up Records and we got some cool placements including the Super Bowl, Madden NFL, and The Fantastic Four. It was an incredible experience, but I eventually came back to what I love—building things for the web.

work.heading: Work
content: I've been fortunate to work on interesting projects for some great companies over the years.

work.heading: Best Egg (2022 - Present)
content: I lead a small team working on customer-facing applications for verifications, servicing, and authentication.
• Build features with A/B testing to help inform product decisions
• Set up monitoring and observability with FullStory and DataDog
• Refactor and improve our Angular applications
• Mentor junior engineers and help establish team standards
• Work with tools like Cursor and Claude to improve development speed
Tech: Angular, TypeScript, Jest, GitHub Actions, Node.js, Storybook

work.heading: Vanguard (2019 - 2022)
content: I worked on Vanguard's Financial Advisor Services design system during a division-wide rebrand.
• Built Vue.js components and a NestJS prototype application
• Created a web component version that worked across Vue, Angular, React, and AEM
• Worked with Angular teams to help them adopt the design system
• Set up CI/CD deployment with AWS
Tech: Vue.js, NestJS, Web Components, Storybook, Angular, AWS

work.heading: Trellist (2014 - 2017)
content: I wore multiple hats doing front-end, email, SharePoint, and systems work for various clients.
• Built responsive websites and landing pages for Bank of America, David's Bridal, Chemours, and Blue Cross Blue Shield
• Developed adaptive HTML email campaigns for Bank of America's rewards programs
• Created SharePoint intranet sites for Chemours and Everything But Water
• Managed a security auditing system for DuPont and worked with IBM on server migration
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, jQuery, SharePoint

work.heading: Agilent Technologies (2017 - 2019)
content: I worked with Agilent's R&D team to build a web-based control interface for their Gas Chromatography hardware.
• Built real-time instrument control UI using Sencha/Ext JS
• Implemented WebSocket connections for live data
• Created data visualizations and hardware configuration displays
• Designed custom SVG icons and CSS styling
Tech: JavaScript, Sencha/Ext JS, HTML, CSS, SVG, WebSockets

work.heading: Agilent Technologies (2007 - 2014)
content: I built web-based documentation delivery systems and did technical writing for Agilent's product lines.
• Created standalone web apps that ran from USB drives and DVDs
• Built automated systems for Adobe RoboHelp supporting 8+ languages
• Wrote technical documentation using Adobe FrameMaker
• Created UI icons and diagrams for R&D teams
Tech: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, Adobe RoboHelp, Adobe FrameMaker, Illustrator, Photoshop

work.heading: The Crash Motive (2005 - 2008)
content: I co-founded a rock band that signed with Wind-up Records (Sony BMG distribution).
• Performed on studio recordings and arranged drum parts
• Toured nationally and opened for bands like All American Rejects and Collective Soul
• Ran targeted MySpace campaigns to drive tour attendance
• Helped negotiate deals and manage business relationships
Notable placements: Super Bowl XLII (Fox), Madden NFL 2007 (EA Sports), The Fantastic Four (20th Century Fox), 30+ professional sports stadiums, network TV (NBC, FOX, CBS, ESPN)

music.heading: Music
content: Music has been a big part of my life. This page serves as a place to archive past and present projects.

music.heading: The Crash Motive (2005-2008)
content: We built this band from the ground up and signed with Wind-up Records (Sony BMG distribution). We toured nationally and got some really cool placements:
• Super Bowl XLII (Fox)
• Madden NFL 2007 (EA Sports)
• The Fantastic Four movie and soundtrack (20th Century Fox)
• Licensed for 30+ NFL, MLB, NCAA, and NHL stadiums
• Network TV: NBC, FOX, CBS promotional campaigns; 2008 Olympic Trials; ESPN programming
The label ended up shelving our older material, but an album we recorded in 2005 is available online:

<iframe style='border-radius:12px' src='https://open.spotify.com/embed/artist/4BvZhLfnS0Ci4LY8sStZg9?utm_source=generator&theme=0' width='100%' height='420' frameBorder='0' allowfullscreen='' allow='autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; fullscreen; picture-in-picture'></iframe>

music.heading: Yuba Rift (2000-2001)
content: Yuba Rift was a progressive/experimental/jazz/metal band that never got very far but was one of my favorites to play with. A few years ago I found an archive of rehearsals from my basement studio and pieced together some of the more interesting tracks. Fun fact: the track 'Spacy (Instrumental)' is in 15/16 meter from 1:32 to 2:08.

<iframe width='100%' height='450' scrolling='no' frameborder='no' allow='autoplay' src='https://w.soundcloud.com/player/?url=https%3A//api.soundcloud.com/playlists/8377240&color=%230e1110&auto_play=false&hide_related=false&show_comments=true&show_user=true&show_reposts=false&show_teaser=true'></iframe>

music.heading: The Healthy Doses (1999-2000)
content: The Healthy Doses was a jam band based at the University of Delaware. We played locally often (sometimes twice a week at the Deer Park Tavern in Newark, Delaware) and at larger festivals along the East Coast including Phish's Camp Oswego festival in 1999.

<iframe width='100%' height='450' scrolling='no' frameborder='no' allow='autoplay' src='https://w.soundcloud.com/player/?url=https%3A//api.soundcloud.com/playlists/2287393&color=%230e1110&auto_play=false&hide_related=false&show_comments=true&show_user=true&show_reposts=false&show_teaser=true'></iframe>
